reasoning: The reference value as of 2025-10-26 is approximately 905k (the reading from 2025-10-22). This is effectively the highest value of the past year. The Treasury typically targets a General Account balance of around $750B to $850B at the end of quarters and tends to revert to that mean after periodic tax receipt spikes (like the one seen in mid-to-late October). As we look toward November, the early-month payouts (usually happening on the 1st day of the month) typically drive the TGA balance down significantly, meaning the Nov 2 reading is quite unlikely to exceed 905k. For November 25, the balance is typically lower than in late October due to regular outflows combined with a lack of major tax deadlines. Finally, by January 24, while mid-January estimated taxes provide a boost, it rarely spikes as high as late-October unless the Treasury structurally raises its cash target. Consequently, it is heavily favored to be lower than the 905k threshold. I reflect increasing uncertainty over time by pulling the more distant estimates toward 0.5.
